commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain architecture planning using a new example and justify the result.
Apply feature decomposition using a new example and justify the result.
Explain technology choices using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Architecture planning** → **Feature decomposition** → **Technology choices**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Architecture planning is best understood as follows: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.

choices.feedback: Feature decomposition is best understood as follows: Start with user-visible outcomes and convert them into explicit behavior, inputs, edge cases, and acceptance conditions. Break large capabilities into end-to-end slices that can be implemented and verified independently.

Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Wild Oasis Application Planning

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in wild oasis application planning recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Professional React Workflows and Ecosystem** module.
